sprint: Sprint 23 -- Ladder Discriminator Round 3 + Cleanup + Lint Hardening
spec: offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-02-from-spool-sprint23-ladder-fix-and-forensic-gaps.md (Drain Test 7 verdict + Spool Stories 1/2/4/5 -> US-275/276/277/278) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-001.md (US-270) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-006-env-example-ollama-base-url.md (US-271) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-040-release-version-seed-test-broken-by-sprint19-close-bump.md (US-272) + offices/pm/issues/I-015 + I-016 + I-017 records (US-273) + offices/pm/action-items.md AI-001 (US-274)
backlogItem: B-043 (Pi auto-sync + conditional-shutdown -- ladder-fires/drain-forensics phases shipped but ladder still does NOT fire post-Drain-7; Round 3 deeper instrumentation phase) + 3 long-lived TDs + 3 stale issue records + AI-001 phantom-path drift.
amendedAt: 2026-05-02 evening (Spool Drain-7 verdict spec received; +4 P0 stories US-275-278)

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-22 close baseline per Rex US-266 verification (3833 PASS / 17 skipped pre-US-268+269; US-268+269 closed TD-042+TD-044 to bring to fully green ~3859). Verify exact count at sprint start. TD-040 closure (US-272) is itself a fast-suite hygiene win (one more red->green).

sprintNotes: GOAL: Drain Test 7 (2026-05-02 18:56-19:15 CDT) was the 7th consecutive hard-crash with full Sprint 22 deployed. The data eliminates H1 (tick thread liveness): US-265 health-check proved tick ran continuously (337 ticks across drain). The data points strongly at H2 (gating logic bails silently): _enterStage was NEVER called despite VCELL crossing all 3 thresholds (3.57V to 3.27V). H3 (write path) UNLIKELY because _enterStage logs at INFO and zero stage-transition log lines appeared. CIO's Pi5 brownout hypothesis DISPROVEN by throttled_hex staying 0x0 the entire drain. What killed the Pi: buck converter dropped 5V regulation at VCELL ~3.30V (LiPo dropout knee, normal hardware behavior). Sprint 23 amends to ship Spool's Round-3 instrumentation (US-275 tick-internal log line capturing every gating decision, US-276 fills the orchestrator state-file writer gap that left US-262 logger's pd_stage/pd_tick_count columns at -1, US-277 auto-wires deploy-pi.sh + adds missing PYTHONPATH env, US-278 documents UPS HAT dropout characteristics). Plus original cleanup (US-270-274). Spool Story 3 (the actual ladder fix) DEFERRED to Sprint 24 -- needs Drain 8 verdict from US-275 instrumentation before specifying.

EXECUTION ORDER (deps-aware): TIER 1-A (P0, ladder discriminator, ship FIRST so Drain Test 8 produces useful data) -- US-275 tick-internal instrumentation + US-276 orchestrator state-file writer + US-277 auto-wire deploy. TIER 1-B (parallel) -- US-278 UPS HAT doc, US-270 TD-001, US-271 TD-006, US-272 TD-040, US-273 records hygiene. TIER 2 -- US-274 sprint_lint file-existence check (the only M).

THEME: 'Round 3 -- find what's silently bailing in tick().' Sprint 21 Round 1 was US-252 (tick decoupled from display); Sprint 22 Round 2 was the discriminator-trio + logger; Sprint 23 Round 3 is deeper internal instrumentation now that we know the tick runs and the write path works. The state-file writer gap (US-276) is THE Sprint-22 ship gap that crippled the logger's diagnostic value -- pd_stage and pd_tick_count have been -1/unknown across Drains 6 + 7. Plus 5 cleanup stories from the original Sprint 23 scope.

CONSTRAINTS (per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md): US-275 + US-276 + US-277 each MUST include a synthetic test that would FAIL pre-fix. US-275's test must mock VCELL stair-step + assert the tick-log line emits with the correct reason field for each early-return case. US-276's test must assert the state file is written + atomic-renamed + readable post-tick. US-277's test must mock systemd unit absent + assert deploy-pi.sh installs + enables. Live drill (Drain Test 8) remains POST-SPRINT action item per CIO no-human-tasks rule.

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool 2026-05-02 sprint23-ladder-fix-and-forensic-gaps inbox note (Drain 7 verdict + Stories 1/2/4/5 specs with acceptance) + Drain 7 CSV at /var/log/eclipse-obd/drain-forensics-20260502T235909Z.csv on Pi + TD-001/006/040 records + I-015/016/017 issue records + offices/pm/action-items.md AI-001 + offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py implementation.

POST-SPRINT-23 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json): (1) CIO + Spool run Drain Test 8 with US-275 + US-276 deployed; Spool reads new tick: log lines + CSV pd_stage/pd_tick_count to identify exact failure mode in tick() gating logic. (2) Spool files Sprint 24 spec for Story 3 (the actual ladder fix) based on Drain 8 evidence -- evidence-driven, not a guess. (3) CIO runs Drive Test 6 cold-start lifecycle (US-260 gate). (4) PM bumps deploy/RELEASE_VERSION V0.22.0 -> V0.23.0 at sprint close (MINOR for new instrumentation surface area + Sprint-22 ship-gap fixes). (5) Stale local sprint branches cleanup (9 now). (6) Optional CIO action: install rsync on Windows MINGW64 to speed up Pi deploy.

stories.title: TD-001 close: TestDataManager pytest collection warning
stories.intent: TD-001 has been open since Sprint 1 (2026-01-29). The `TestDataManager` class in `tests/test_utils.py` has a `Test` prefix, so pytest tries to collect it as a test class but can't instantiate due to constructor args, emitting a warning every test run. Two valid fixes per TD remediation: (a) rename to `DataManager` (disruptive -- breaks all imports), (b) add `__test__ = False` class attribute (one-line, no caller impact). Pick (b); least-disruption per CIO no-over-engineering directive.

Any caller of TestDataManager (no rename, no API change)

groundingRefs.value: TD-001 open since 2026-01-29; pytest warning every run

stories.title: AI-001 close: sprint_lint.py file-existence check on filesToTouch
stories.intent: 9-session pattern of phantom paths in `sprint.json` `scope.filesToTouch` (Sprints 14-22; latest example Sprint 22 US-264 listed `src/pi/display/dashboard_layout.py` but actual path is `src/pi/hardware/dashboard_layout.py`). Marcus's PM-side template-generator drift; Ralph wastes audit time tracking down the real path on every occurrence. Fix: extend `offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py` with a check that walks every story's `scope.filesToTouch`, parses out the path (strips parenthetical commentary), and verifies UPDATE paths exist in the current repo. NEW paths (annotated with `(NEW ...)` in the parenthetical) are exempt -- they're SUPPOSED to not exist yet.

stories.title: Tick-internal instrumentation: log every gating decision (Round 3)
stories.intent: Drain Test 7 proved tick thread runs continuously (337 ticks across 16-min drain) but `_enterStage` was NEVER called despite VCELL crossing all 3 thresholds (3.57V to 3.27V). Sprint 22 US-266 added DEBUG-level early-return logs but they don't render at default INFO level + don't capture the FULL decision context per tick. This story adds ONE INFO-level log line per battery tick capturing the complete decision-relevant state, so post-Drain-8 Spool can read journalctl + identify exactly which gating mode is silently bailing. The discriminator: if `willTransition=False reason=threshold_not_crossed` while VCELL is clearly below threshold -> comparison-logic bug (sign error / units mismatch); `vcell_none` or `power_source!=BATTERY` while we KNOW we're on battery -> state-caching bug; `willTransition=True` repeatedly but no `_enterStage` log -> state-machine bug (decision made but action not taken).

filesToTouch: src/pi/power/orchestrator.py (UPDATE -- in tick(), after each guard or at the end-of-decision point, emit ONE logger.info("PowerDownOrchestrator.tick: vcell=%.3f currentStage=%s thresholds=%s willTransition=%s reason=%s", ...) line capturing the per-tick state. reason field values: power_source!=BATTERY | vcell_none | already_at_stage | threshold_not_crossed | OK. Only emit when on BATTERY (no log spam when on AC).)

tests/pi/power/test_tick_internal_instrumentation.py (NEW -- parametrized test driving I2cClient.readWord stair-step + power-source mock through 5 reason cases; asserts exactly one INFO log line per tick with the expected reason field for each scenario; would FAIL pre-fix because the new log line does not exist)
filesToRead: src/pi/power/orchestrator.py (post-Sprint-22 PowerDownOrchestrator.tick + US-266 DEBUG instrumentation -- add this NEW INFO log alongside, do NOT remove DEBUG)

stories.title: Wire orchestrator state file: PowerDownOrchestrator writes for logger
stories.intent: Sprint 22 US-262 logger reads `/var/run/eclipse-obd/orchestrator-state.json` for `pd_stage` and `pd_tick_count` columns BUT the writing side was NEVER built -- those columns have been -1/unknown across Drains 6 and 7. Critical Sprint-22 ship gap. Fix: PowerDownOrchestrator writes the state file on every tick via atomic-rename pattern (write to tmp, rename to final). Schema: `{tickCount, currentStage, lastTickTimestamp, lastVcellRead, powerSource}`. Once shipped, drain-forensics CSV columns finally populate with real values + the discriminator analysis from US-275 has a complementary CSV trail.

stories.title: Auto-wire forensic logger systemd in deploy-pi.sh + PYTHONPATH fix
stories.intent: Sprint 22 US-262 left systemd-unit install as a manual operator post-deploy hook; Spool had to run sudo cp + systemctl daemon-reload + enable mid-Drain-7 because deploy-pi.sh did not auto-install drain-forensics.service + .timer. Plus the unit ships WITHOUT `Environment=PYTHONPATH=/home/mcornelison/Projects/Eclipse-01` so the script no-ops because UpsMonitor import fails (Spool patched the live /etc/systemd/system/ copy but the repo file was unchanged). This story auto-wires both: idempotent install step in deploy-pi.sh + adds PYTHONPATH to deploy/drain-forensics.service.

stories.title: UPS HAT dropout characteristics doc (Spool knowledge)
stories.intent: Drain Test 7 produced empirical UPS HAT data: buck converter loses 5V regulation around VCELL ~3.30V (LiPo dropout knee); Pi5 ran 16 minutes on battery under typical load (Pi5 idle, BT scan, HDMI display, ~0.06-0.42 load avg, 38-40 degC ambient, throttled_hex stayed 0x0). Spool requests this be captured as a knowledge doc referenced from MEMORY.md, useful for future tuning + car-wiring scope decisions (US-169 / US-189 / US-190).

filesToTouch: offices/tuner/knowledge/ups_hat_dropout_characteristics.md (NEW -- empirical Drain 7 measurements: dropout knee VCELL value, runtime under typical load, CPU temp range, throttled_hex behavior, load avg range; reference to Drain 7 CSV path on Pi; cross-link to US-169/US-189/US-190 future scope)
MEMORY.md (UPDATE -- power-mgmt section adds reference to the new knowledge doc; minimal addition under existing MAX17048 + UPS sections)
filesToRead: MEMORY.md (current power-mgmt + UPS sections for cross-link placement)
